title: nicolasia pedunculata subsp. thermalis
intro: Nicolasia pedunculata subsp. thermalis, also known as the Thermal Nicolasia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Nicolasia pedunculata subsp. thermalis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain pathogens. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
